questions.question: Which type of error prevents a Java program from compiling?
questions.answer: Syntax error

questions.question: A Java program compiles and runs without crashing, but produces incorrect output. What type of error does it contain?
questions.answer: Logic error

questions.question: What exception is thrown when you attempt to access an array index that does not exist?
questions.answer: ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException

questions.question: In a Java stack trace, which line should you read first to find where the error originated?
options: The very first line, which names the exception
The very last line of the trace
The line immediately after 'Exception in thread'
The top 'at' line directly below the exception message
questions.answer: The top 'at' line directly below the exception message

options: An ArithmeticException is thrown at runtime
questions.answer: An ArithmeticException is thrown at runtime

questions.question: Which of the following is a syntax error?
options: Using > instead of >= in a comparison
Missing a semicolon at the end of a statement
questions.answer: Missing a semicolon at the end of a statement

questions.question: What exception is thrown when you call a method on a null reference in Java?
questions.answer: NullPointerException

questions.question: Which debugging technique involves explaining your code step by step to an inanimate object or another person?
questions.answer: Rubber duck debugging

questions.question: What happens when Integer.parseInt("twelve") is executed?
questions.answer: A NumberFormatException is thrown

questions.question: A for loop intended to count down from 5 to 1 uses i++ instead of i--. What type of error is this?
questions.answer: Logic error

questions.question: What is the consequence of a recursive method that lacks a base case?
options: A StackOverflowError is thrown at runtime
The method runs once and stops
questions.answer: A StackOverflowError is thrown at runtime

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a common debugging strategy?
options: Adding print statements to trace execution flow
Deleting code until the program compiles
Testing with edge cases and boundary values
questions.answer: Deleting code until the program compiles

options: The closing brace } is missing before else
The if statement needs parentheses around the body
questions.answer: The closing brace } is missing before else

questions.question: In Java, what does the for-each loop syntax look like for iterating over an int array called scores?
questions.answer: for (int score : scores)

questions.question:
What is the error in this loop condition?

for (int i = 0; i <= 3, i++)
options: The loop variable should start at 1
The comma should be a semicolon separating the condition and update
questions.answer: The comma should be a semicolon separating the condition and update

questions.question: Why does scores.length() cause a compile-time error when scores is an int array?
options: Arrays do not have a length() method; the correct syntax is scores.length
The method should be called scores.size()
length() only works on String arrays
You must cast scores to Object first
questions.answer: Arrays do not have a length() method; the correct syntax is scores.length

questions.question: What type of error is it when a program uses values[i] < max instead of values[i] > max to find the maximum value in an array?
questions.answer: Logic error

options: Multiplying any number by 0 always gives 0, so the result is always 0
The variable should be declared as long
The *= operator is not valid in Java
questions.answer: Multiplying any number by 0 always gives 0, so the result is always 0

questions.question: An array has 5 elements. What happens when a for loop uses the condition i <= numbers.length instead of i < numbers.length?
options: The loop skips the last element
An ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException is thrown on the last iteration
questions.answer: An ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException is thrown on the last iteration

questions.question: Which of the following correctly checks if a number is between 10 and 20 (inclusive)?
questions.answer: if (number >= 10 && number <= 20)

options: The semicolon after the if condition creates an empty if body, so the block always executes
grades[i] should be cast to double
questions.answer: The semicolon after the if condition creates an empty if body, so the block always executes

questions.question:
What kind of error results from integer division when a decimal result is expected?

double classAverage = sum / grades.length;
options: Syntax error — the types are incompatible
Logic error — integer division truncates the decimal portion
No error — Java automatically produces a double result
questions.answer: Logic error — integer division truncates the decimal portion

questions.question: In a prime-number checker, what is the bug if isPrime is set to true (instead of false) when num % i == 0?
options: The program crashes with an ArithmeticException
Composite numbers are incorrectly identified as prime
questions.answer: Composite numbers are incorrectly identified as prime

questions.question: Which approach is most effective for finding logic errors that do not produce exceptions or crashes?
options: Adding print statements to trace variable values during execution
Running the program with a larger heap size
questions.answer: Adding print statements to trace variable values during execution
